name: winning
description:
Jack Welch's Winning — an executable toolkit for business leadership: how to build a culture of candor, differentiate your people, manage talent rigorously, and drive your organization to outperform.

Covers 5 use cases:
① Building a Culture of Candor — create an environment where people speak openly and the best ideas win
② Talent Management & Differentiation — identify, develop, reward top performers; address low performers with honesty
③ Mission & Values Alignment — define a clear mission that drives daily decisions
④ Strategy & Execution — the 5-slide strategy framework for clarity and action
⑤ Leading Through Change — manage acquisitions, restructurings, and organizational change.

Trigger when users say: "How to be a better leader" "Jack Welch" "Winning" "Business management" "Leadership strategy" "Managing people" "Performance management" "Organizational culture" "How to give feedback" "Business strategy" "Candor at work" "Differentiation" "20-70-10" "GE management"
or mention: Jack Welch / Winning / candor / differentiation / GE / leadership / talent management / mission and values.

Related skills: the-essential-drucker, leadership-in-turbulent-times, the-four-steps-to-the-epiphany, the-outsiders, clear-thinking-book.
useCase: Helps leaders at all levels build high-performance organizations through candor, talent differentiation, clear strategy, and relentless execution.
knownRisks: Welch's 20-70-10 differentiation approach has been criticized as harsh; adapt the principle of honest performance management to your organizational culture.
